items.title: Bill restricting late-term abortions passes SA upper house

children.content: South Australia's upper house has passed a bill to restrict access to abortions past 25 weeks. The legislation passed 10 votes to 9 with the support of three newly-elected One Nation MPs.

articles.title: MP joins conservative Family First party ahead of abortion bill debate

children.content: SA upper house MP Sarah Game has abandoned the political party she founded to join the conservative Family First brand, marking the third time she has changed parties in less than two years.

image.altText: A woman speaks to microphones while a man looks on

gallery.altText: A woman in a black west with a green and blue ribbon pinned to her chest walks up stone steps.

thumbnail.altText: A woman in a black west with a green and blue ribbon pinned to her chest walks up stone steps.

items.title: New SA laws would remove serious offenders' rights over victims' remains

children.content: The South Australian government will introduce legislation to remove the rights of perpetrators of serious crimes to decide what happens with the remains of their victims.

image.altText: A woman with long brown hair wearing a black top holds a photograph of a young man and woman

items.title: Pastoralists take to the skies to track livestock lost in floods

children.content: Closed roads and flooded plains are making livestock inaccessible for some pastoralists, while others are employing some serious technology to track down their animals.

image.altText: A small sheep up to its knees in dirty water and mud

items.title: 'Thou shalt not kill, I have sinned': Jury hears alleged parent murder confession

children.content: Accused murderer Raelene Polymiadis was allegedly captured on police listening devices confessing to killing her elderly parents while praying, a court has heard.

gallery.altText: A woman with shoulder length hair and sunglasses.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with shoulder length hair and sunglasses.

avatar.alt: A woman with blonde hair wearing a black blazer and white top

items.title: Roadside reptile mystery continues as remains confirmed as saltwater croc

children.content: Reptile remains found dumped on the side of an Adelaide roadside have been confirmed as belonging to a saltwater crocodile, but there is still no explanation for how they came to be there.

image.altText: crocodile with its mouth open on a muddy river bank.

items.title: SA Electoral Commissioner resigns amid review into March poll

children.content: South Australia's Electoral Commissioner Mick Sherry has resigned amid a review into the scandal-plagued state and Voice elections.

image.altText: A man in a suit and tie against a colourful background.

items.title: Man arrested after allegedly assaulting four cops, biting two, during arrest

children.content: A man has been arrested after he allegedly assaulted a woman and a group of police officers in regional South Australia.

image.altText: A red brick building with police cars parked in front of it

items.title: Two more SA hospitals pause birthing services amid staff shortage

children.content: Two more South Australian regional centres are now temporarily diverting expectant mothers as Kapunda Hospital's birthing service is closed indefinitely.

image.altText: A hospital building with a ramp leading to the entrance and a white roof

items.title: 'Reasonable possibility' father injected insulin himself, murder trial jury told

children.content: A woman standing trial accused of murdering her parents with insulin has denied the allegations, while her defence lawyer has told the jury the possibility one of the alleged victims injected a fatal dose of insulin himself cannot be ruled out.

image.altText: A woman with shoulder length hair and sunglasses.

items.title: Cancer patient in remission after 'amazing' outcome of targeted trial

children.content: Ben Whitehead was given just months to live after being diagnosed with a rare cancer that doctors had not seen before. A second clinical trial has delivered hope.

image.altText: Profile of a man sitting on a bench.

items.title: 'Ecosystem engineers' returned to desert decades after local extinction

children.content: Wiped off mainland Australia by the introduction of cats and foxes, burrowing bettongs are making a triumphant comeback in outback NSW.

image.altText: A baby Burrowing Bettong being held in the hands of Dr Rebecca West at the Wild Deserts project

items.title: Algal bloom information 'inconsistent', amid survey of health impacts of the bloom

children.content: Volunteers surveyed more than 200 people who reported a range of symptoms during the algal bloom outbreak not represented in the SA government's health advice. A disability advocate says health information has been "inaccessible" and "inconsistent".

image.altText: A mother and son stand in front of an ocean looking at the camera

items.title: Messi, Mbappé and Haaland shine as stars come out to play

children.content: Lionel Messi scored a hat-trick, while Kylian Mbappé and Erling Haaland both scored braces on a night where the World Cup's biggest stars shone brightest.

articles.title: Iraqi scores goal in World Cup debut after being detained by US customs

children.content: Iraq striker Aymen Hussein was delayed on the way to his first World Cup, but he still made the best of his tournament debut.

image.altText: Aymen Hussein cleebrates a goal for Iraq at 2026 World Cup.

articles.title: Quick hits: Mbappé's stunning goal and why this coach refused to pose

children.content: Kylian Mbappé's magical strike thrust him back into the World Cup spotlight, while master coach Marcelo Bielsa did everything he could to avoid it. French journalists leave an empty space and Jesus gets a Mexican kit makeover. Here's the five quick hits from the FIFA World Cup.

image.altText: Kylian Mbappé, Roberto Lopes and Marcelo Bielsa composite.

articles.title: Socceroo's blunt reply to USA's claim at World Cup

children.content: Socceroos defender Alessandro Circati offers a blunt reply to Sebastian Berhalter's comments ahead of the World Cup clash between the two sides.

image.altText: Alessandro Circati celebrates after Socceroos' win over Türkiye at FIFA World Cup.

items.title: 'ISIS bride' accused of owning a slave in Syria refused bail

children.content: A woman accused of slavery offences while supporting the Islamic State group in Syria has had her bail application refused.

image.altText: A court sketch of Zeinab Ahmad

items.title: Jeremy Clarkson reveals 'aggressive' cancer diagnosis

children.content: The former Top Gear host shared the news on an episode of his Clarkson's Farm reality series, shortly before undergoing surgery that removed "10 per cent" of his prostate.

image.altText: An older man in a chequered shirt leans back in his chair with a grim expression on his face.

items.title: Trump comes face to face with the targets of his ire at G7

children.content: In France, the US president finds himself among a group of emboldened counterparts, many of whom now seem more comfortable criticising him publicly than ever before.

articles.title: A close look at Iran's 14-point plan reveals what Tehran could gain

children.content: Iran agreeing to a ceasefire deal with the United States tells us Tehran likely had something to gain from signing on. This is what Iran is promoting as the 14-point deal.

image.altText: An image of Donald Trump in front of a map of Iran.

articles.title: Trump intensifies pressure on Netanyahu over Lebanon attacks

children.content: The US president levels more criticism at Israel's prime minister and suggests asking Syria to take on Hezbollah "if Israel can't do the job without killing everyone else".

image.altText: Donald Trump stands in front of a 'G7 France' sign.

articles.title: Farmer fertiliser hope as Iran ceasefire promises to reopen supply

children.content: Farmers are hoping the US-Iran deal will help free up fertiliser supply and lower costs during the winter cropping season.

image.altText: A hand holds brilliant white fertiliser while a truck tips it off in the background

items.title: Kyle Sandilands responds to his $12m settlement with ARN Media

children.content: In an ASX announcement on Wednesday, ARN Media agreed to pay the shock jock $12.09 million, with $3 million payable as soon as next month.

image.altText: Shock jock Kyle Sandilands addresses media.

items.title: Two children die after being struck by car

children.content: Two children have died after being struck by a car while sitting in a pram in Sydney's south-west.

image.altText: An aerial photo of a road where ambulances and police cars are parked.

items.title: Glue Store closes after posting $8.4m loss as economic pain hits retailers

children.content: Glue Store announces its permanent closure, becoming the latest Australian retailer to fail this year.

image.altText: A closed sign on a Melbourne business.

items.title: Business jet crashes on Texas highway leaving one person dead

children.content: The crash caused chaos as people left their vehicles to frantically try to smash the cockpit window and free those inside.

image.altText: People attempt to pull passengers out of a plane after it crashed on a highway

items.title: What happened when Ann Patchett quit swearing for good

children.content: The bestselling author of books including Bel Canto, Tom Lake and her latest, Whistler, is determined to see the good in everyone.

gallery.altText: Ann Patchett, a woman with short greying hair, wearing a floral dress, sitting in front a series of bookshelves.

thumbnail.altText: Ann Patchett, a woman with short greying hair, wearing a floral dress, sitting in front a series of bookshelves.

items.title: After 11 years on a housing wait list, Lynette is getting desperate

children.content: As the number of people waiting for social housing continues to grow, the amount of available homes is falling as a proportion of Australia's total housing stock.

image.altText: Woman wearing glasses sitting on a red couch

items.title: Six gaming experts share their favourite video games of the year so far

children.content: From cosy builders to hectic shooters and all the way through to nostalgic adventures, there are lots of gems to enjoy on your favourite game console so far this year.

image.altText: Three people shaped characters stand with a pokemon trubbish

items.title: Father of 17yo killed in e-bike crash speaks after teen driver sentenced

children.content: A Gold Coast father whose son died in an electric bike crash has spoken out after the driver was sentenced, saying the devices need better regulation.

items.title: Bluey speaks Indigenous language in time for NAIDOC week

children.content: The animated award-winning cartoon series Bluey has been translated into Yolŋu Matha, from north-east Arnhem Land, for NAIDOC Week.

image.altText: Bluey is sitting at the beach playing with a shell.

items.title: Runaway Tasmanian devil Mary recaptured after two-week search

children.content: A spokesperson for Village Roadshow Theme Parks said Mary, the two-year-old marsupial, was found and recaptured overnight.

image.altText: Tasmanian devil Mary CCTV

items.title: Minister denies vomit gesture, says he was moving a breath-freshening strip

children.content: A Tasmanian Liberal politician denies he made a vomit gesture while listening to a female MP speak about integrity matters — saying he was moving a breath-freshening strip that had become stuck to the roof of his mouth.

image.altText: Kristie Johnston speaks in parliament as Gavin Pearce sits behind her and puts fingers in his mouth.

items.title: Gina Rinehart's iron ore company flags job losses, reportedly in the hundreds

children.content: Gina Rinehart's Hancock Iron Ore has cut jobs a year after merging its two iron ore businesses into a single entity, but won't say how many.

image.altText: Gina Rinehart stands at a podium.

items.title: Cambodia says 310,000 suspected scammers left country amid crackdown

children.content: A report by Amnesty International casts doubt on the impact of Cambodia's high-profile scam industry crackdown, finding authorities failed to dismantle the "vast majority" of operations.

thumbnail.altText: A drone shot of a prison-like compound with a series of long buildings with blue roofs surrounding a square centre building.

items.title: AFL stars' mother recounts visit from Dezi Freeman detectives

children.content: Kay Reid, a regional Victorian businesswoman and the mother of footballers Ben and Sam Reid, confirms police searched her home as part of Taskforce Summit's investigation into the movements of police killer Dezi Freeman.

image.altText: A police officer stands near two others who sit at a table with a bald man in a green shirt who look directly at the camera.

items.title: CFMEU accused of 'dark strategy' to generate millions in 'illegitimate' revenue

children.content: An inquiry into alleged union misconduct and criminal links in Queensland hears the CFMEU weaponised workplace agreements to bolster its revenue.

image.altText: Exterior photos of a CFMEU building.

items.title: New Zealanders become first World Cup mother-son duo

children.content: When Tyler Bindon came off the bench for New Zealand in its men's World Cup opener against Iran, he made a bit of history with his mother.

articles.title: England enjoying 'right to dream' about winning World Cup

children.content: As a World Cup favourite, England is pushing aside decades of heartbreak and disappointment to embrace the pressure of finally trying to bring football home again.

image.altText: England players train together in Dallas at 2026 World Cup.

articles.title: The night Scotland painted Boston's baseball heartland in tartan

children.content: Throughout the famed but understated bleachers of Boston's Fenway Park, an army of Scottish football fans created a night few fans will ever forget.

items.title: Two men charged over alleged kidnapping murder of Sydney grandfather

children.content: Robbery and Serious Crime Squad detectives have charged another two men following an investigation into the alleged kidnapping and murder of Chris Baghsarian earlier this year in Sydney's north.

image.altText: A young man with his head in his hands sitting on the ground, three officers standing either side of him.

items.title: What a trillion dollars costs democracy

children.content: Democracy cannot survive trillionaires. We need to start taxing billionaires properly.

image.altText: Elon Musk wearing a suit behind a dark backdrop.
